NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On an eight-stringed harp. A Psalm of David. Help, LORD, for the godly man ceases! For the faithful disappear from among the sons of men.
NKJV.2: They speak idly everyone with his neighbor; With flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
NKJV.3: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, And the tongue that speaks proud things,
NKJV.4: Who have said, “With our tongue we will prevail; Our lips are our own; Who is lord over us?”
NKJV.5: “For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, Now I will arise,” says the LORD; “I will set him in the safety for which he yearns.”
NKJV.6: The words of the LORD are pure words, Like silver tried in a furnace of earth, Purified seven times.
NKJV.7: You shall keep them, O LORD, You shall preserve them from this generation forever.
NKJV.8: The wicked prowl on every side, When vileness is exalted among the sons of men.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Sheminith<S>8067</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Help<S>3467</S>, LORD<S>3068</S>; for the godly man<S>2623</S> ceaseth<S>1584</S>; for the faithful<S>539</S> fail<S>6461</S> from among the children<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S>. <sup>upon: or, upon the eighth</sup> <sup>Help: or, Save</sup>

KJV.3: The LORD<S>3068</S> shall cut off<S>3772</S> all flattering<S>2513</S> lips<S>8193</S>, and the tongue<S>3956</S> that speaketh<S>1696</S> proud<S>1419</S> things: <sup>proud: Heb. great</sup>
KJV.4: Who have said<S>559</S>, With our tongue<S>3956</S> will we prevail<S>1396</S>; our lips<S>8193</S> are our own: who is lord<S>113</S> over us? <sup>are: Heb. are with us</sup>
KJV.5: For the oppression<S>7701</S> of the poor<S>6041</S>, for the sighing<S>603</S> of the needy<S>34</S>, now will I arise<S>6965</S>, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; I will set<S>7896</S> him in safety<S>3468</S> from him that puffeth<S>6315</S> at him. <sup>puffeth: or, would ensnare him</sup>

ESV.1: Save, O LORD, for the godly one is gone; for the faithful have vanished from among the children of man.
ESV.2: Everyone utters lies to his neighbor; with flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
ESV.3: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, the tongue that makes great boasts,
ESV.4: those who say, “With our tongue we will prevail, our lips are with us; who is master over us?”
ESV.5: “Because the poor are plundered, because the needy groan, I will now arise,” says the LORD; “I will place him in the safety for which he longs.”
ESV.6: The words of the LORD are pure words, like silver refined in a furnace on the ground, purified seven times.
ESV.7: You, O LORD, will keep them; you will guard us  from this generation forever.
ESV.8: On every side the wicked prowl, as vileness is exalted among the children of man.

NASB.1: For the choir director; upon an eight-stringed lyre. A Psalm of David. Help, LORD, for the godly man ceases to be, For the faithful disappear from among the sons of men.
NASB.2: They speak falsehood to one another; With flattering lips and with a double heart they speak.
NASB.3: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, The tongue that speaks great things;
NASB.4: Who have said, 'With our tongue we will prevail; Our lips are our own; who is lord over us?'
NASB.5: 'Because of the devastation of the afflicted, because of the groaning of the needy, Now I will arise,' says the LORD; 'I will set him in the safety for which he longs.'
NASB.6: The words of the LORD are pure words; As silver tried in a furnace on the earth, refined seven times.
NASB.7: You, O LORD, will keep them; You will preserve him from this generation forever.
NASB.8: The wicked strut about on every side When vileness is exalted among the sons of men.

NLT.1:
Help, O LORD, for the godly are fast disappearing!
The faithful have vanished from the earth!
NLT.2:
Neighbors lie to each other,
speaking with flattering lips and deceitful hearts.
NLT.3:
May the LORD cut off their flattering lips
and silence their boastful tongues.
NLT.4:
They say, “We will lie to our hearts’ content.
Our lips are our own — who can stop us?”
NLT.5:
The LORD replies, “I have seen violence done to the helpless,
and I have heard the groans of the poor.
Now I will rise up to rescue them,
as they have longed for me to do.”
NLT.6:
The LORD’s promises are pure,
like silver refined in a furnace,
purified seven times over.
NLT.7:
Therefore, LORD, we know you will protect the oppressed,
preserving them forever from this lying generation,
NLT.8:
even though the wicked strut about,
and evil is praised throughout the land.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician; upon an eight-stringed lyre. A Psalm of David. Help, Yahweh; for the godly man ceases. For the faithful fail from among the children of men.
WEB.2: Everyone lies to his neighbor. They speak with flattering lips, and with a double heart.
WEB.3: May Yahweh cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that boasts,
WEB.4: who have said, “With our tongue we will prevail. Our lips are our own. Who is lord over us?”
WEB.5: “Because of the oppression of the weak and because of the groaning of the needy, I will now arise,” says Yahweh; “I will set him in safety from those who malign him.”
WEB.6: Yahweh’s words are flawless words, as silver refined in a clay furnace, purified seven times.
WEB.7: You will keep them, Yahweh. You will preserve them from this generation forever.
WEB.8: The wicked walk on every side, when what is vile is exalted among the sons of men.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: according to . ⓚ A psalm of David. [1] Help, LORD, for no faithful one remains;
the loyal have disappeared from the human race. [1] ⓛ
CSB17.2:
They lie to one another;
they speak with flattering lips and deceptive hearts. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
May the LORD cut off all flattering lips
and the tongue that speaks boastfully. ⓝ
CSB17.4:
They say, “Through our tongues we have power;
our lips are our own — who can be our master?” ⓐ
CSB17.5:
“Because of the devastation of the needy
and the groaning of the poor,
I will now rise up,” says the LORD.
“I will provide safety for the one who longs for it.” ⓑ
CSB17.6:
The words of the LORD are pure words,
like silver refined in an earthen furnace,
purified seven times. ⓒ

you will protect us [3] from this generation forever. ⓓ
CSB17.8:
The wicked prowl [4] all around,
and what is worthless is exalted by the human race. ⓔ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set an octave below. A Psalm of David. Save and help and rescue, LORD, for godly people cease to be,
For the faithful vanish from among the sons of men.
AMP.2:
They speak deceitful and worthless words to one another;
With flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
AMP.3:
May the LORD cut off all flattering lips,
The tongue that speaks great things [in boasting];
AMP.4:
Who have said, "With our tongue we will prevail;
Our lips are our own; who is lord and master over us?"
AMP.5:
"Because of the devastation of the afflicted, because of the groaning of the needy,
Now I will arise," says the LORD; "I will place him in the safety for which he longs."
AMP.6:
The words and promises of the LORD are pure words,
Like silver refined in an earthen furnace, purified seven times.
AMP.7:
You, O LORD, will preserve and keep them;
You will protect him from this [evil] generation forever.
AMP.8:
The wicked strut about [in pompous self-importance] on every side,
As vileness is exalted and baseness is prized among the sons of men.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Quick, God, I need your helping hand!
The last decent person just went down,
All the friends I depended on gone.
MSG.2:
Everyone talks in lie language;
Lies slide off their oily lips.
They doubletalk with forked tongues.
MSG.3:
Slice their lips off their faces! Pull
The braggart tongues from their mouths!
MSG.4:
I'm tired of hearing, "We can talk anyone into anything!
Our lips manage the world."
MSG.5:
Into the hovels of the poor,
Into the dark streets where the homeless groan, God speaks:
"I've had enough; I'm on my way
To heal the ache in the heart of the wretched."
MSG.6:
God's words are pure words,
Pure silver words refined seven times
In the fires of his word-kiln,
Pure on earth as well as in heaven.
MSG.7:
God, keep us safe from their lies,
From the wicked who stalk us with lies,
MSG.8:
From the wicked who collect honors
For their wonderful lies.

NIV.1:
Psalm 12
For the director of music. According to sheminith. A psalm of David.
Help, Lord, for the godly are no more;
the faithful have vanished from among men.
NIV.2:
Everyone lies to his neighbor;
their flattering lips speak with deception.
NIV.3:
May the Lord cut off all flattering lips
and every boastful tongue
NIV.4:
that says, “We will triumph with our tongues;
we own our lips—who is our master?”
NIV.5:
“Because of the oppression of the weak
and the groaning of the needy,
I will now arise,” says the Lord.
“I will protect them from those who malign them.”
NIV.6:
And the words of the Lord are flawless,
like silver refined in a furnace of clay,
purified seven times.
NIV.7:
O Lord, you will keep us safe
and protect us from such people forever.
NIV.8:
The wicked freely strut about
when what is vile is honored among men.

NRSVCE.1:
Help, O LORD, for there is no longer anyone who is godly;
the faithful have disappeared from humankind.
NRSVCE.2:
They utter lies to each other;
with flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
NRSVCE.3:
May the LORD cut off all flattering lips,
the tongue that makes great boasts,
NRSVCE.4:
those who say, “With our tongues we will prevail;
our lips are our own — who is our master?”
NRSVCE.5:
“Because the poor are despoiled, because the needy groan,
I will now rise up,” says the LORD;
“I will place them in the safety for which they long.”
NRSVCE.6:
The promises of the LORD are promises that are pure,
silver refined in a furnace on the ground,
purified seven times.
NRSVCE.7:
You, O LORD, will protect us;
you will guard us from this generation forever.
NRSVCE.8:
On every side the wicked prowl,
as vileness is exalted among humankind.

NET.1: Deliver, LORD! For the godly have disappeared; people of integrity have vanished.
NET.2: People lie to one another; they flatter and deceive.
NET.3: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that boasts!
NET.4: They say, “We speak persuasively; we know how to flatter and boast. Who is our master?”
NET.5: “Because of the violence done to the oppressed, because of the painful cries of the needy, I will spring into action,” says the LORD. “I will provide the safety they so desperately desire.”
NET.6: The LORD’s words are absolutely reliable. They are as untainted as silver purified in a furnace on the ground, where it is thoroughly refined.
NET.7: You, LORD, will protect them; you will continually shelter each one from these evil people,
NET.8: for the wicked seem to be everywhere, when people promote evil.
